tree.setup: The airport departure lounge, bright and echoey. You've passed through security and realized you have no water. A small convenience shop glows nearby. The shopkeeper, Xiao Chen, is arranging bottles of water by brand with the intensity of a chess player. Each row is exactly even.
tree.setup_zh: 机场候机大厅，明亮又有回音。过了安检以后你发现没有水了。旁边有一个小便利店。店员小陈正在按品牌排列矿泉水瓶，专注得像在下棋。每一排都整整齐齐。

options.feedback: Xiao Chen selects a bottle from the front row -- the one with the most aesthetically pleasing label placement, naturally. 瓶 (píng) is the measure word for bottles.
「八块。」Airport prices. Xiao Chen shrugs apologetically -- he doesn't set the prices, he just arranges the bottles. 多少钱 is your constant companion.
Xiao Chen gestures at the two sizes. Small or big. There is no 'cheap' in an airport -- only 'less expensive.' He understands the question though. 便宜 means 'cheap' or 'inexpensive.'

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Decide if you need anything else.

options.feedback: Xiao Chen rings you up and immediately turns back to his bottle arrangement. One has shifted during the transaction. This cannot stand.
Xiao Chen points at a small shelf of individually wrapped snacks, arranged -- of course -- by color. 吃的 turns the verb 'eat' into a noun: 'something to eat.'
Xiao Chen blinks. He sells water. He does not know your flight schedule. But he gently points at the departure board across the hall.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_pinyin: hǎo de. zhù nǐ lǚtú yúkuài!

tree.cultural_note: Airport prices in China (机场价格) are higher than outside -- especially for water and snacks. Many travelers bring empty bottles and refill at free hot water stations (热水, rè shuǐ). 祝你旅途愉快 is a formal travel blessing.
